design_name: A 90-degree pipe elbow with annular cross-section swept along a quarter-circle arc in the XZ plane. Outer diameter 20mm, inner diameter 16mm, 2mm wall thickness. Bend centerline is a 40mm radius quarter-circle arc from origin heading +Z, curving to (40,0,40) heading +X.
prompt: A 90-degree pipe elbow with the bend in the XZ plane. The pipe has a 20mm outer diameter and 16mm inner diameter (2mm wall thickness). The bend centerline follows a quarter-circle arc of 40mm radius on the XZ plane: starting at the origin heading in the +Z direction, curving through the first quadrant, and ending at (X=40, Z=40) heading in the +X direction. The pipe cross-section is centered on this arc. The part is centered at Y=0. Created by sweeping the annular pipe cross-section along the arc path defined by a radiusArc from (0,0) to (40,40) with radius 40 on the XZ workplane.
primitive_plan_summary: A 90-degree pipe elbow with annular cross-section swept along a quarter-circle arc in the XZ plane. Outer diameter 20mm, inner diameter 16mm, 2mm wall thickness. Bend centerline is a 40mm radius quarter-circle arc from origin heading +Z, curving to (40,0,40) heading +X.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The pipe elbow is correctly constructed as a 90-degree bend in the XZ plane. Bounding box dimensions of 50×20×50mm are correct: the bend centerline arc has radius 40mm, plus the pipe outer radius of 10mm on each side gives 50mm in X and Z, and 20mm in Y (the outer diameter). The part starts near Z=0 and extends to Z=50, starts near X=-10 and extends to X=40, consistent with the arc from origin to (40,40) with 10mm pipe radius. The volume of ~7106 mm³ is plausible for a quarter-torus annular section (theoretical: π/2 × R × (r_outer² - r_inner²) = π/2 × 40 × (100-64) ≈ 2262 mm³... actually let me recalculate: V = π²/2 × R × (r_o² - r_i²) = (π²/2) × 40 × 36 ≈ 7106 mm³, which matches exactly). The rendered images confirm: isometric view shows a proper 90-degree elbow with visible hollow ends, the rear view shows the curved pipe shape, and the front profile view clearly shows the annular cross-section with correct wall thickness. The part is watertight, valid, with 4 faces (inner surface, outer surface, two annular end faces) and 6 edges as expected. Centered at Y=0 confirmed by center of mass Y ≈ 0.
